name: m16.3-b4-paro-ffn-megakernel-cb
verdict: megakernel REGRESSES C_B as wired; do not enable by default
hardware: AMD Radeon Pro W7900 (gfx1100 / RDNA3), 48 CUs
model: /models/hipengine/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-PARO-full4096-e5-packed-MTP-BF16 (fp16 activations/theta/scales; hidden=2048, moe_intermediate=512, E=256, top_k=8, krot=8, 40 layers)
note_decode_tokens: decode-tokens capped at 8 because the spec-decode baseline diverges from AR at ~token 10 in BOTH proposal impls with the gate OFF (pre-existing, not the megakernel); <=8 keeps exact_ar_match=true for a clean comparison.
cb_definition: C_B = (verify_seconds*1000 / target_forward_calls) / (1000 / ar.decode_tok_s)  [verify-cycle cost in AR-token-equivalents]

configs.desc: Python fallback, unfused FFN (dispatch off, gate off)
PARO FFN megakernel (gate on; bypasses C dispatcher)
configs.fires_breakdown: 40 MoE layers x 6 verify forward calls

configs.desc: microbench synchronized per-call latency (krot=8, c=4, hot cache, isolated)
configs.caveat: synchronized per-call latency vs the NAIVE unfused chain; does NOT predict streamed in-cycle cost or compare vs production fused staged-keyed selected kernels

correctness: exact_ar_match=true in A, B, and C (megakernel produces byte-identical verified tokens). On-model stacked qweight/qzeros/scales + int64 selected_experts + krot=8 rotation layouts all validated. KL fused-vs-unfused ~1.7e-5.
finding: The fused PARO FFN megakernel, wired into run_moe_c1_fp16 and firing in all 40 MoE layers, RAISES the verify-cycle C_B from ~4.4 (production C dispatcher) to ~5.3 (+20%). Bypassing the C dispatcher is not the cause (config B Python-fallback-unfused is ~42 ms, ~= the dispatcher's ~41 ms). The megakernel kernel itself is ~7-9 ms/cycle slower than the production selected path.
root_cause_hypothesis: (1) The B3 microbench measured SYNCHRONIZED per-call latency at tiny shapes (dominated by fixed launch/sync overhead) and compared vs the NAIVE unfused chain; in the STREAMED verify cycle kernels are pipelined (no per-call sync), so that overhead -- and the 5->1 launch collapse -- is immaterial (config B ~= A). (2) The megakernel's block-per-(token,expert) design launches only rows=32 blocks on 48 CUs (occupancy-starved) and runs the whole FFN serially per block, while the production multi-kernel selected path parallelizes each stage across output-packs x rows and fills the GPU. At the verify shape, GPU fill beats launch count.
recommended_next: Re-baseline the microbench against the PRODUCTION fused staged-keyed selected kernels (gemv_awq_selected_dual_pack8_transposed_rotate_staged_keyed_fp16 + selected silu_rotate_staged_keyed + staged down), not the naive unfused chain.
Measure per-kernel in-cycle durations via rocprofv3 on one verify cycle (gate on vs off) to confirm the occupancy/fill hypothesis.

If pursued: redesign the megakernel for more parallelism at the verify shape (split-K or multiple blocks per (token,expert) with a reduction) so it fills 48 CUs; only then can it beat the production selected path in-cycle.
Keep HIPENGINE_PARO_FFN_MEGAKERNEL default OFF until it demonstrably lowers in-cycle C_B.
